entries.value: Target food cost ratio is 28-35% of revenue. Above 35% indicates waste, over-portioning, or pricing issues. Below 28% may signal quality concerns. Fine dining can run 30-38%, fast casual should stay under 30%.

Target 2-3 table turns per service for casual dining. Fine dining targets 1-1.5 turns. Lunch service should turn faster (45-60 min) than dinner (75-90 min). Low turnover = slow service or poor reservations management.
Average check (revenue per guest) is the primary revenue lever. Track separately for food and beverage. Beverage attachment rate target is 60-70%. Upselling can increase average check by 15-25%.

Food waste should stay below 5% of food purchases. Track prep waste, plate waste, and spoilage separately. Prep waste above 3% suggests menu engineering issues. Plate waste above 2% indicates portion size problems.

No-show rate should stay below 10%. Above 15% requires intervention: confirmation calls, credit card holds, or overbooking strategy. Track by day-of-week and source channel (phone vs online vs third-party).
Total labor cost (including benefits) should be 25-35% of revenue. Front of house 12-18%, back of house 10-14%, management 3-5%. Combined food + labor (prime cost) must stay under 65%.
Beverage cost ratio target: liquor 18-20%, beer 24-28%, wine 30-35%. Blended beverage cost should be 20-25%. Pour cost above 25% on spirits indicates over-pouring or theft.

Classify items into four quadrants: Stars (high profit, high popularity), Plowhorses (low profit, high popularity), Puzzles (high profit, low popularity), Dogs (low profit, low popularity). Promote Stars, reprice Plowhorses, reposition Puzzles, remove Dogs.

Maintain at least 2 suppliers per critical ingredient category. Review supplier pricing quarterly. Negotiate volume discounts for top-5 spend items. Track delivery reliability (on-time %) and quality rejection rate.

A 'cover' is one guest served during a service period. 'Daily covers' = total guests across all services. Used to calculate average check (revenue / covers) and labor efficiency (covers per labor hour).
